hostels_and_facilities.hostel_allotment_note: Official pages publish capacity but do not provide a current 2026 blanket guarantee. Allotment rules and availability must be confirmed for the admission batch.
hostels_and_facilities.mess: Hostel messes are managed and regulated by the MNIT Mess Council; 2026-27 mess payment is an advance adjusted at actual.
hostels_and_facilities.wifi: Digital infrastructure is officially listed; exact hostel reliability is not officially quantified.
hostels_and_facilities.gym: Gym/sports facilities are referenced in official institute material; current access rules and timings require verification.
hostels_and_facilities.sports: Official sports facility page exists; exact inventory was not fully extracted.
hostels_and_facilities.laboratories: Departmental laboratories exist; programme-level inventory not fully normalized.
hostels_and_facilities.medical_centre: On-campus primary-care dispensary with medical officers, two full-time nursing staff and visiting specialists; published weekly timings available.
hostels_and_facilities.counselling_centre: Student welfare/wellness mechanisms are institutionally listed; exact 2026 service schedule requires verification.
hostels_and_facilities.transport: Public buses, taxis and autos connect the campus; institute travel page mentions bus routes 7, 17 and an airport connection.
hostels_and_facilities.shops: Shopping complex, post office and canteen officially listed.
hostels_and_facilities.accessibility: PwD seats and institutional cells are present; detailed campus-accessibility audit not found.
hostels_and_facilities.security: Hostel rules and anti-ragging mechanisms are linked on official pages; current curfew details were not verified.

location.college_name: Malaviya National Institute of Technology Jaipur
location.official_address: Jawahar Lal Nehru Marg, Malviya Nagar, Jaipur, Rajasthan 302017, India
location.nearest_major_hospital: Jaipur Hospital / major Tonk Road hospitals (map check required)
location.technology_ecosystem: Jaipur urban ecosystem; Sitapura industrial area approximately 15 km by road; institute incubation and industry-interaction units on campus.
location.cost_of_living_band: Moderate for a major Indian city; inferred, not an official college figure
location.data_origin: Official address and transport pages plus approximate map-derived coordinates/distances

senior_junior_culture.summary: No sufficiently repeated, recent and independently verifiable evidence was found to characterize senior-junior relations college-wide. Official anti-ragging and student-welfare mechanisms exist, but that does not establish lived experience.

academic_culture.summary: A recent first-year AMA mentions proxy attendance in some subjects, but the available student evidence is too limited to characterize attendance strictness, grading or faculty accessibility across departments.
positive_themes: Large programme and department ecosystem may provide varied academic opportunities, based on official structure rather than student ratings.
negative_themes: Limited anecdotal concern about uneven classroom attendance enforcement.

hostel_reality.summary: Visual tours and one first-year AMA suggest newer/first-year accommodation can be functional, while another public discussion reports a hostel-allotment dispute. Conditions likely vary by hostel and year; published capacity is not an allotment guarantee.
positive_themes: Newer hostel rooms and common areas are visible in public tours.
A first-year student described the first-year hostel as decent.
negative_themes: Hostel quality may vary by building.
A 2024 dispute raised concerns about allotment and delivery restrictions.

club_and_campus_life.summary: Official evidence shows a broad and recently active club ecosystem, including large 2026 cultural events. Student-vlog evidence also shows active campus spaces, but independent student assessment of selection fairness and first-year access is limited.
positive_themes: Official Cultural Society lists 18 active clubs.
Science and Technology Society lists 13 technical/innovation clubs.
Youthfest 2026 drew broad participation according to the institute newsletter.
negative_themes: No robust independent evidence on selection fairness or access for first-year students.
club_and_campus_life.evidence_strength: Moderate institution-published signal; limited independent student evidence

coding_and_placement_culture.summary: Multiple recent public discussions characterize coding culture as self-driven rather than strongly institution-led, while official records show active technical clubs and substantial software/technology recruiter presence. The evidence therefore suggests opportunity exists, but peer intensity may vary and students should not assume a uniformly strong coding environment.
positive_themes: Official Computer Science, Data Science, Infosec, Robotics and Entrepreneurship clubs exist.
Recent official reports list major technology recruiters.
negative_themes: Several recent student comments describe coding culture as weaker than applicant expectations.
Non-CSE access to specific software roles is not officially published.
coding_and_placement_culture.evidence_strength: Moderate repeated student-reported signal with institution-published counter-evidence

locality_and_student_life.summary: The campus is within Jaipur and close to railway and airport links according to official pages. Public tours indicate an active residential campus, but recent independent evidence on evening transport, food costs and off-campus PG experience is limited.
positive_themes: Urban Jaipur location rather than a remote campus.
Multiple rail stations and the airport are relatively close by official estimates.
negative_themes: Exact travel times vary with traffic.
Nearest metro and current late-evening public transport convenience were not reliably verified.
locality_and_student_life.evidence_strength: Limited anecdotal signal supported by official location facts

overall_satisfaction.summary: Public comments are materially mixed: some describe lively college life and adequate first-year facilities, while others criticize coding culture, placement trends or administrative policies. The evidence is not strong enough to generalize a single satisfaction verdict.
positive_themes: Lively campus and social life appears in first-person and visual sources.
negative_themes: Concerns about coding culture and placement volatility.
Hostel/administrative policy concerns in a dispute context.

parent_mode_summary.classification: Perspective summary based on collected evidence
strengths: Public Institute of National Importance with 2026 JoSAA intake of 948.
Income/category-based tuition remission can materially lower cost.
Three recent official branch-wise placement reports are available.
Urban Jaipur location with nearby rail and airport links.
Large published on-campus hostel capacity and primary-care dispensary.
watch_items: Hostel allotment is not guaranteed by capacity figures.
Mess is billed at actual against advances.
Current accreditation is unclear for many branches.
Placement percentage uses registered students, not all graduating students.
Scholarships require separate applications and are not guaranteed.
recommended_checks: Confirm final 2026 fees and hostel allotment.
Use branch medians and three-year placement trend.
Verify current accreditation and scholarship deadlines.
Plan travel from the relevant campus gate/hostel.

student_mode_summary.classification: Perspective summary based on collected evidence
student_mode_summary.branch_fit: Eleven 2026 UG programmes are available. Engineering Physics and Mathematics and Computing have no 2023-2025 cutoff history in the supplied dataset, so compare curriculum and career goals rather than relying on predictions.
student_mode_summary.coding_and_core_culture: Official technical clubs and major technology recruiters are present, but recent student comments repeatedly describe coding as self-driven. Core-branch students should confirm software-role eligibility.
student_mode_summary.campus_life: The institute publishes a broad club ecosystem and recent large cultural events. Public tours show a large residential campus; independent evidence on club-selection fairness is limited.
student_mode_summary.hostel_and_city: Hostel experience appears to vary by building. Jaipur access is a strength, but current allotment and rules should be checked.
student_mode_summary.preparation_advice: Start with branch fundamentals, DSA/programming or core tools in year one, join active clubs, seek internships early and use branch-specific placement trends rather than headline packages.

decision_risks.risk: Historical cutoffs are not official 2026 cutoffs.
decision_risks.mitigation: Use them only for trend bands and update after official 2026 rounds.

decision_risks.risk: Engineering Physics and Mathematics and Computing lack historical cutoff/placement records in the supplied period.
decision_risks.mitigation: Do not extrapolate branch-specific ROI from older branches without clear assumptions.

decision_risks.risk: Software-focused students in core branches may face branch-dependent role eligibility and a self-driven coding environment.
decision_risks.mitigation: Verify recruiter eligibility, build CS fundamentals early and use technical clubs/projects.

decision_risks.risk: Placement rates fluctuate and use registered-student denominators.
decision_risks.mitigation: Compare branch medians and registered/placed counts across all three years; do not rely only on highest package.

decision_risks.risk: Hostel capacity does not guarantee allotment.
decision_risks.mitigation: Confirm 2026 allotment policy and retain an off-campus contingency.

decision_risks.risk: Mess amount is an advance adjusted at actual.
decision_risks.mitigation: Budget with a contingency rather than treating ₹25,000 per semester as a final fixed cost.

decision_risks.risk: Most programme-level NBA information is outdated.
decision_risks.mitigation: Verify current certificates before using accreditation in comparisons.
Check income/category rules and official deadlines; never treat a match as guaranteed.

decision_risks.risk: Student-experience evidence is conflicting and may not represent all batches.
decision_risks.mitigation: Speak with multiple current students from the exact branch and hostel before deciding.

data_gaps: Official 2026 JoSAA/CSAB cutoffs do not yet exist because counselling is not completed; 2023-2025 are historical only.
11 JoSAA 2024 rows in the supplied export have blank opening and closing ranks and are excluded from numeric prediction inputs.
Engineering Physics and Mathematics and Computing appear in the 2026 seat matrix but do not have 2023-2025 rows in the supplied cutoff dataset.
Current NBA status is not verified for most UG programmes; the institute-hosted status PDF is dated August 2022.
NAAC grade/score and current validity were not verified from a current authoritative record.
AICTE institute ID/approval status was not asserted because NIT statutory treatment and current approval requirements require authoritative confirmation.
Placement reports publish registered and placed counts, not graduating/eligible counts, and do not separate domestic from international highest package.
Official recruiter role, eligible-branch, selection-count and package matrices are not published in the reviewed reports.
Hostel room occupancy, current curfew, laundry availability and 2026 allotment guarantee were not verified.
Nearest metro and current late-evening public transport were not reliably verified.
Independent student-experience evidence is limited and uneven across branches, hostels and batches.
Cycle-specific 2026-27 scholarship deadlines and renewal rules must be checked on the relevant scheme portals.
